title: Morning Light
author: Original Work
lyrics:
The morning light comes streaming in,
Through windows high and wide,
I rise to greet another day,
With hope I cannot hide.

The birds are singing on the wing,
Their melodies so free,
I'll join their chorus, clear and strong,
And find my own key.

Each new day brings chances bright,
For happiness and grace,
I'll seize the moments as they come,
And run my daily race.

title: River's Journey
author: Original Work
lyrics:
The river flows from mountain high,
To valleys far below,
It carries on its gentle song,
Wherever it may go.

The fish swim deep in waters cool,
The reeds bow low and sway,
The river knows no boundaries,
It finds its own way.

Like water flowing free and wild,
Our spirits too can roam,
From troubles dark to brighter shores,
We always find our home.

title: Stars Above
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Stars above shine bright tonight,
Like diamonds in the sky,
I think of you beneath their light,
And wonder how time flies.

Your eyes reflect the cosmos deep,
Your voice like zephyr breeze,
When I'm with you I feel complete,
At perfect ease.

The stars will fade at break of dawn,
But love remains anew,
Forever shining just like them,
In skies of midnight blue.

title: The Wanderer's Return
author: Original Work
lyrics:
I've traveled roads both long and worn,
Through storms and sunny days,
Now homeward bound my heart does turn,
To familiar ways.

The inn I left so long ago,
The garden by the door,
My family waits with open arms,
As they have before.

No gold nor glory called me back,
Just love I hold so true,
The wanderer's return at last,
To lives I've always knew.

title: Autumn Leaves
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Autumn leaves in golden hue,
Are falling all around,
The summer's warmth has passed away,
With winter's approach found.

The trees stand bare against the sky,
So dignified and tall,
They wait for spring to bring again,
New growth upon them all.

Like leaves that fall and turn to earth,
We too shall return,
To nourish new beginnings strong,
And lessons we have learned.

title: Dancing in the Rain
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Let the rain come down on me,
I'll dance beneath the sky,
Each drop that falls washes clean,
The troubles from my eye.

The puddles bounce beneath my feet,
The thunder rolls afar,
I'm dancing free and wild and loud,
Like a shooting star.

The storm will pass, the sun will shine,
And I will still be here,
Dancing through the rain and shine,
With nothing held in fear.

title: Mountain High
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Stand tall against the mountains high,
Where eagles make their nest,
Your spirit soars above the clouds,
With courage manifest.

No valley low can hold you back,
No ocean stop your way,
The mountain peak awaits your climb,
Beginning today.

From summit high the view's so grand,
All fears seem far behind,
You touched the sky and found your wings,
And left your chains unwind.

title: Firelight Stories
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Gather round the firelight,
Where shadows dance and play,
We share our tales of wonder,
Of yesterday and today.

The embers glow like rubies red,
The sparks ascend on high,
Each story told brings warmth within,
As hours pass us by.

The fire dies but memories,
Burn bright within our heart,
Of firelight and stories told,
That never really part.

title: City Lights
author: Original Work
lyrics:
City lights like stars below,
Illuminate the night,
I walk these streets you're not on now,
And hold your memory tight.

The taxi cabs pass yellow bright,
Like schools of fish at sea,
You're somewhere near yet far away,
From this urban spree.

The city that never sleeps,
Now lullaby does sing,
Of loneliness without your hand,
And the peace your voice would bring.

title: Sea of Dreams
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Sail away on sea of dreams,
Where imagination roams,
To islands far in distant realms,
That live within our homes.

The tides pull strong on hearts of men,
To harbors yet unknown,
The compass spins to point the way,
Through waters not yet shown.

Our voyage long on dreaming sea,
Brings treasures rare to find,
The real treasures we discover,
Are treasures of the mind.

title: Midnight Train
author: Original Work
lyrics:
On the midnight train I ride,
Through darkness all alone,
The rhythm of the railway ties,
My weary heart to home.

The lights of towns flash past the windows,
Like shooting stars above,
Each station passed brings me closer,
To the one I love.

The journey's long but soon I'll see,
The platform where you wait,
And hold you close,再也不分离,
At last we're not too late.

title: Heart's Reflection
author: Original Work
lyrics:
In the mirror I see your face,
Where once was only mine,
Now every feature that I trace,
Holds love within its line.

The reflection shows a brighter me,
Since you became a part,
Of all the dreams I'm dreaming now,
And all that's in my heart.

You're the reflection of my soul,
In glass so crystal clear,
The truest love I have ever known,
Is standing somewhere near.

title: Garden's Song
author: Original Work
lyrics:
In my garden sweet and small,
The flowers bloom and grow,
The roses red, the lilies white,
A rainbow's gentle glow.

The bees do buzz from bloom to bloom,
The butterflies dance free,
The garden sings its song of life,
For all the world to see.

And as I tend my garden plot,
With love I sow and reap,
I find that peace grows deeper still,
When roots are buried deep.

title: Moonlit Path
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Walk with me beneath the moon,
On silver lit retreat,
Where shadows dance in moonlit trance,
And shadows softly meet.

The nightingale does serenade,
From branches dark and high,
While we two walk the moonlit path,
Beneath the starry sky.

The moon does guide our wandering feet,
To wonders yet unseen,
On paths of light we walk tonight,
In love's eternal dream.

title: Old photographs
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Old photographs in faded box,
Tell stories we forgot,
Of holidays and birthday parties,
That time has since ungrokked.

The faces young now lined with years,
The smiles now turned to tears,
But love that shined through every frame,
Endures beyond our fears.

I'll frame these memories today,
And hang them on my wall,
A testament to love gone by,
That helped me stand up tall.

title: First Born
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Little one with eyes so bright,
You bring us such delight,
Our family is complete,
With hands and feet so neat.

You laugh and coo and always smile,
Make every trial worthwhile,
Our hearts burst with pride,
Our new one by our side.

We'll guide you every day,
Through work and through your play,
To be the best you can be,
Set free.

title: Homestead
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Four walls that hold our love so true,
Make any house a home,
With you beside me every day,
No place I'd rather roam.

The fireplace crackles warm and bright,
The porch swing sways with ease,
Our homestead built on solid ground,
With every season's breeze.

This land is ours to call our own,
Where two hearts beat as one,
A sanctuary from the world,
Until our days are done.

title: Simple Days
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Simple days are sweetest days,
With coffee and the news,
No grand adventures far away,
Just potted plants and shoes.

The morning light through curtains thin,
A comfortable chair,
A book half read beside the bed,
A life beyond compare.

Give me simplicity and peace,
With nothing grand or new,
The simple days are treasure troves,
When shared with only you.

title: Sailing Home
author: Original Work
lyrics:
The sails are set, the wind is fair,
For harbor lights ahead,
After months upon the sea,
I'm dreaming of my bed.

The gulls cry out their welcome home,
As shore draws ever near,
The harbor bell rings clear and strong,
To touch my waiting ear.

The anchor drops, the sails come down,
I've sailed so far from home,
But nothing feels as sweet as this,
To never more roam.

title: Final Hour
author: Original Work
lyrics:
When my final hour arrives,
And I must say goodbye,
I pray I'll face it without fear,
Beneath the open sky.

I hope to have no regrets,
No sorrow for the days,
That passed me by without a trace,
Or veered from chosen ways.

But if I fail, dear Lord above,
Please welcome me anew,
A prodigal soul returning home,
Whose love was always true.

title: Wildflower Field
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Wildflowers grow in field and meadow,
Where nobody tends or sows,
They find their way through rock and soil,
Against all common shows.

Purple, yellow, white, and blue,
A painter's palette free,
The wilding bloom cares nothing for,
What humans think to be.

I'd rather be a wildflower bold,
Than garden rose refined,
Growing free where winds do blow,
In body, spirit, mind.

title: Farewell to Summer
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Summer sun begins to fade,
The days grow short and cool,
The vacation memories made,
Live on within our school.

The beach days done, the campfire's out,
The sandcastle's washed away,
But golden tan and laughter loud,
Will stay through winter's gray.

We'll hold the sunshine in our hearts,
Till summer comes again,
When warm winds blow and life restarts,
We'll welcome joy and pain.

title: Morning Coffee
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Morning coffee steams so sweet,
The first sip wakes me up,
The newspaper lies flat and neat,
A morning ritual cup.

The radio plays soft and low,
The birds begin to sing,
The sun creeps in through window glow,
A brand new day to bring.

These simple pleasures make me whole,
Contented, calm, and clear,
A cup of coffee warms my soul,
And guides my day with cheer.

title: Letter From Afar
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Your letter came from overseas,
With stamps and postmarks new,
The words you wrote so long ago,
Still ring so tried and true.

You asked about my health and work,
And how the garden grew,
The children tall, the oldest's smart,
Just like you said they'd do.

Though miles divide us, still your words,
Bridge oceans but by sea,
A lifeline stretched across the world,
From someone dear to me.

title: Grandfather's Clock
author: Original Work
lyrics:
Grandfather's clock stands tall and proud,
Its pendulum swings wide,
Each tick and tock marks time's own road,
Where secret hopes reside.

It watched us grow from children small,
To adults with our own,
It marked our triumphs, failures, all,
Through every season's tone.

The clock may have wound down now,
Its brass has turned to green,
But time stands still in memory,
Where love still lingers clean.

title: Dreams We Carry
author: Original Work
lyrics:
We carry dreams from youth to age,
In pockets deep and wide,
Some dreams have faded with the years,
While others still our guide.

The child who dreamed of flying high,
Now finds a different sky,
The girl who dreamed of dancing far,
Now spins in place nearby.

But dreams don't die, they just evolve,
To fit our present true,
A dream of happiness and love,
Is always born anew.

title: Stormy Weather
lyrics:
Storm clouds gather overhead,
The thunder rolls so near,
I weather every tempest strong,
Through all I hold most dear.

The lightning strikes, the rain descends,
Yet shelter I have found,
For those who love stand by my side,
On this their consecrated ground.

title: Cemetery Gates
lyrics:
We gather at the gates of stone,
Where loved ones rest so deep,
The marble angels watch us weep,
And flowers eternally sleep.

The names are worn by time's own hand,
Yet memory clings on strong,
We come to honor those who've passed,
To keep their love where it belongs.

title: Country Road Home
lyrics:
Down the country road I ride,
My horse beneath the moon,
The crickets chirp their evening song,
A gentle summer tune.

The fences line both sides of road,
Where fireflies dance and play,
I'm heading home to those I love,
At end of every day.

title: Sweet Sixteen
lyrics:
She turned sweet sixteen today,
A lady fair and fine,
The boy next door brought flowers round,
And asked if she were kind.

They danced beneath the paper lanterns,
Twirling round the floor,
Two young hearts learning love's first steps,
As they've done before.

title: Empty Nest
lyrics:
The house feels bare since you've flown,
Where laughter once did ring,
The rooms echo with memories,
Of when you spread your wing.

But pride replaces sorrow deep,
For those who learned to soar,
You found your sky beyond our walls,
And that's what love is for.

title: Harvest Moon
lyrics:
The harvest moon hangs big and bright,
On fields of golden grain,
The farmers work through moonlit nights,
To bring in winter's gain.

The combines hum from dusk to dawn,
The silos fill up tall,
Another year of plenty's come,
To feed both great and small.

title: Old Dog's Day
lyrics:
My old dog lies beside the porch,
His bones grow stiff and weak,
He chases squirrels in his dreams,
Though legs no longer seek.

The puppy days have passed him by,
But love remains so true,
A faithful friend through many years,
Still loyal through and through.

title: Train Whistle Moan
lyrics:
The train whistle moans through the night,
A lonely sound so clear,
It's carrying the wanderers,
To destinations near.

The railroad tracks stretch far and wide,
From town to town they go,
The iron horse that roars so loud,
Brings travelers to and fro.

title: Cobblestone Streets
lyrics:
Old cobblestone streets lead to the square,
Where market vendors call,
The smell of bread and flowers sweet,
Fills both great hall and small.

The fountain's spray catches the light,
As children run and play,
This village square has stood for years,
Where life goes on today.

title: Letter to Tomorrow
lyrics:
Dear tomorrow, I'm writing you,
To ask what's in your plan,
Will sunshine follow rain today,
Or will I finally understand?

I fold this letter carefully,
And seal it with my hope,
The future holds a special place,
Where dreams learn how to grope.

title: Rainy Day Blues
lyrics:
The rain beats down on window pane,
The world has turned to gray,
I've got those rainy day blues,
That never seem to go away.

But coffee steams beside my book,
A blanket soft and warm,
Sometimes the rainy days are best,
To shelter from the storm.

title: New Year's Bells
lyrics:
Ring out the bells for New Year's Eve,
Let joy fill every heart,
We say goodbye to year gone by,
And welcome a new start.

The champagne bubbles sparkle bright,
The ball descends so slow,
A thousand voices cheer as one,
As out the old does go.

title: Butterfly Garden
lyrics:
The butterflies drift through the garden,
On wings of orange bright,
They flutter past the rosebushes,
In their graceful, lazy flight.

The children chase but never catch,
These treasures of the air,
A living rainbow flits about,
With beauty beyond compare.

title: Night Owl's Song
lylysics:
I'm an owl that hoots at midnight,
When the world has gone to bed,
I see the stars from my old oak tree,
And hear the thoughts we've said.

The mice may scurry, worms may squirm,
But I just sit and wait,
For dawn to bring the morning light,
When I shall fly to fate.

title: Sleigh Ride Winter
lyrics:
Jingle bells ring through crisp white snow,
Our sleigh rides swift and light,
The horse hooves pound a steady rhythm,
As we bundle up in fur.

The winter wind it nips our nose,
But warmth comes from within,
When shared with friends and loved ones dear,
The cold cannot begin.
